name: Bahrain
intro: Bahrain punches above its size in financial services, and its banks, insurers and fintech ventures run constant change programmes — core system replacements, digital onboarding, regulatory upgrades. That steady change portfolio keeps demand for certified project managers and Scrum Masters healthy in Manama, while the causeway economy also feeds roles serving Saudi programmes. Bahraini employers follow formal HR frameworks, and PMP or a Scrum credential is usually the screening line for PMO and delivery positions. We run live online cohorts throughout the year on Bahrain-friendly hours — weekend Saturday–Sunday batches and midweek Wednesday–Thursday batches both — meaning learners in Bahrain attend at ordinary local times, never overnight; the table below shows each upcoming date converted for Bahrain. Instruction is in English with an international cohort, and invoices are provided for employer sponsorship. After the workshop we support your exam application, provide realistic mock exams, and remain available for questions until you clear the exam.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Bahrain?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Bahrain?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Bahrain.
